title: Welcome to School Game Zone
source: Generated from approved unit runtime and linked master dictionary

games.title: Meaning Match
games.description: Match each unit word to its approved child-friendly meaning.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: A flat top with legs where we work or eat.
rounds.explanation: table: A flat top with legs where we work or eat.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: The colour of a tomato, a strawberry or a fire engine.
rounds.explanation: red: The colour of a tomato, a strawberry or a fire engine.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: A grown-up who helps you learn new things at school.
rounds.explanation: teacher: A grown-up who helps you learn new things at school.

games.title: Spelling Builder
games.description: Choose letter tiles to build important unit words.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: A seat with a back and four legs for one person.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: The colour of the sky on a sunny day.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: Someone you like, play with and are kind to.

games.title: Sentence Puzzle
games.description: Put unit words into the correct sentence order.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: We put our books on the table.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: I read a book every night.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.

games.title: Language Choice
games.description: Choose the form that makes the unit language accurate.

rounds.prompt: Which sentence tells someone how old you are?
rounds.explanation: Only this sentence gives an age; the others give a name, name a thing, or tell what you like.

rounds.prompt: Musa is holding up a pencil to show the class. What should he say?
rounds.explanation: He is showing a pencil, so he names that thing; the other sentences do not describe what he is holding.

rounds.prompt: Which sentence tells something you are able to do?
rounds.explanation: The word can tells what you are able to do; the other sentences only name things or a person.

games.title: Reading Detective
games.description: Use unit evidence to solve each reading clue.

rounds.prompt: What is the name of the boy who smiles at Amal?
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: Adam

rounds.prompt: Amal spoke softly. Does softly mean loudly or quietly?
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: Quietly

rounds.prompt: How did Amal feel when Adam asked to be her friend?
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: Happy

games.title: Speaking Quest
games.description: Listen, record, review and submit useful unit language.

rounds.prompt: Speaking 1 - Listen and point
rounds.target: Point to school things as an adult names them, then name them yourself.

rounds.prompt: Speaking 3 - Ask and answer
rounds.target: I read a book every night.

rounds.prompt: Speaking 5 - Role-play
rounds.target: Play teacher and pupil with a partner.

games.title: Word Order Race
games.description: Arrange unit language in a clear, accurate order.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
Put this unit sentence in order.
Put this unit sentence in order.

games.title: Definition Dash
games.description: Choose the approved meaning before moving to the next clue.

rounds.prompt: What does 'green' mean in this unit?
choices: The colour of grass and of leaves on a tree.
A flat top with legs where we work or eat.
A big white board the teacher writes on with special pens.
rounds.answer: The colour of grass and of leaves on a tree.
rounds.explanation: green: The colour of grass and of leaves on a tree.

rounds.prompt: What does 'boy' mean in this unit?
choices: A flat top with legs where we work or eat.
A big white board the teacher writes on with special pens.
A young male child, like a brother or a school friend.
rounds.answer: A young male child, like a brother or a school friend.
rounds.explanation: boy: A young male child, like a brother or a school friend.

rounds.prompt: What does 'whiteboard' mean in this unit?
choices: Pages joined together with stories, pictures or words to read.
A big white board the teacher writes on with special pens.
A flat top with legs where we work or eat.
rounds.answer: A big white board the teacher writes on with special pens.
rounds.explanation: whiteboard: A big white board the teacher writes on with special pens.

games.title: Word Type Power
games.description: Identify how each vocabulary word works in English.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'white'?
rounds.explanation: white is used as a adjective in this dictionary sense.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'sing'?
rounds.explanation: sing is used as a verb in this dictionary sense.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'ruler'?
rounds.explanation: ruler is used as a noun in this dictionary sense.

games.title: Memory Pairs
games.description: Reveal tiles and connect each word with its meaning.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 1.
pairs: A big white board the teacher writes on with special pens.
Pages joined together with stories, pictures or words to read.
A coloured wax stick that we use for colouring pictures.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 2.
pairs: The colour of grass and of leaves on a tree.
A colour made from red and blue, like a grape.
A light colour made from red and white, like a flower.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 3.
pairs: A young male child, like a brother or a school friend.
To make letters and words with a pencil or pen.
To keep quiet and hear carefully with your ears.

games.title: Question Quest
games.description: Read each unit question carefully and choose the best answer.

rounds.prompt: Which word begins with the same sound as book?
rounds.explanation: Book and bag both start with /b/; pen starts with /p/, cup with /c/ and mat with /m/.

rounds.prompt: What do you say to your teacher when you arrive at school in the morning?
rounds.explanation: We greet people in the morning with Good morning; good night and goodbye are for leaving.

rounds.prompt: Which sentence tells someone your name?
rounds.explanation: Only this sentence gives a name; the others tell an age, point at a thing, or tell what you can do.

games.title: Unit Mission
games.description: Finish with a mixed challenge across the whole unit.

rounds.prompt: Which word names something you find in your classroom?
rounds.explanation: A whiteboard is in the classroom; a camel, a river and a cloud are all found outside.

rounds.prompt: Which classroom thing do you sit on?
rounds.explanation: We sit on a chair; a book is for reading, a clock shows the time and a ruler helps us measure.

rounds.prompt: Which classroom thing tells you the time?
rounds.explanation: A clock shows the time; a crayon colours, a table holds our work and a door opens and closes.
